mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with HOUST/RSAFS growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals recovering manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show negative correlation with INDPRO momentum in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in CPI above 2.5% triggers inflation expectations that prompt central bank rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show positive correlation with CPI spikes in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) below its 12-month average signals recession risks that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show positive correlation with GDP declines in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market that drives consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns show negative correlation with PAYEMS/UNRATE co-movement in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.05).
